LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MASSACHUSETTS MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SAN MATEO COUNTY IS DENIED. SAN FRANCISCO IS A PROPER VENUE AS TO MASS MUTUAL BECAUSE IT IS A FOREIGN CORPORATION WHO HAS NOT DESIGNATED A PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AND CONSEQUENTLY IS SUBJECT TO VENUE IN ANY CALIFORNIA COUNTY. (HOBSON V. METROPOLITAN (1931) 114 CAL.APP. 349, 355.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Terrence J. Coleman of the law offices of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP (415.433.8000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Linda B. Oliver of the law offices of Maynard Cooper & Gale (415.646.4707) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Massachusetts Mutual Life Insurance Co.

Deputy Attorney General Heather Brae Hoesterey of the California Department of Justice (415.703.5506) appears as counsel for defendant, Dave Jones, California Insurance Commissioner

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MASSACHUSETTS MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Transfer Venue to San Mateo County is DENIED. San Francisco is a proper venue as to Mass Mutual because it is a foreign corporation who has not designated a principal place of business in California and consequently is subject to venue in any California county. (Hobson v. Metropolitan (1931) 114 Cal.App. 349, 355.) Further, venue is proper in San Francisco County because the Insurance Commissioner, in his official capacity, resides in San Francisco County. (Insurance Code 12905.) CCP 393(b) does not displace CCP 395 because the complaint alleges a failure to act on the part of the Insurance Commissioner. (California State Parks Foundation v. Superior Court (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 826, 834.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.
